name: From Portets to Bordeaux
description:
The south of the Gironde is full of slopes and singles just waiting to be explored. In 20 minutes by train, you are already far from Bordeaux and only have to think about the return trip. On the left bank of the Garonne, the drop is relatively flat and you can enjoy this outing at the pace of a walk through the pines at the start and then the vines finally.


When you get off the train, you go through the vineyards towards Saint-Selve, then enjoy a shaded road under the pines. The roads being mainly straight lines, keep a close eye on the route to exit and join the first gravel portion. This leads first to a DFCI and then to a small path that crosses a small stream. Once out of this path, you arrive at the town of Civrac and take the road towards La Brède, if you feel like it, there are small cafes and terraces that will allow you to enjoy the outdoors during beautiful days.

Small difficulty, leaving La Brède, you go through a small road that climbs a little, the advantage is that this road is not very busy compared to the EuroVélo 3 which could take you to Bordeaux. You cut through the forest to reach the Route de la Solitude, the road on which the Domaine de la Solitude is located, a Pessac-Léognan renowned in the region.

You then head towards Martillac to take the old road that leads you to Bordeaux. You arrive at the station where you can either get back on the train or set off to explore another route offered in this Collection.
region_intro: Gravel biking around Portets offers diverse terrain through vineyards, extensive forests, and along the Garonne River. The region features gentle elevation changes, making it accessible for various activity levels. The Canal de Garonne provides well-maintained paths, often connecting to older railway lines, ideal for gravel grinding experiences. This blend of natural landscapes and historical routes creates varied scenery for cycling enthusiasts. Best gravel bike trails around Portets The most popular gravel bike trail is From Portets to Bordeaux, a 22.3 miles (35.9 km) trail that takes 1 hour 57 minutes to complete. This easy route follows the Garonne River, offering scenic views. Another top favourite among local gravel bikers is From Cérons to Bordeaux, a difficult 29.9 miles (48.1 km) path. This route features varied terrain through the Graves appellation vineyards and along the river. Local gravel bikers also love the Old railroad line – Church in La Brède loop from Saint-Médard-d'Eyrans, a 33.9 miles (54.6 km) trail leading through forests and agricultural areas, often completed in about 3 hours 30 minutes. Gravel biking around Portets is defined by vineyard paths, extensive forests, and routes along the Garonne River and Canal de Garonne. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside rides to more challenging routes through varied landscapes. The routes in Portets are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 11 reviews. More than 40 gravel bikers have used komoot to explore Portets's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on gravel bike trails around Portets?
A: The Portets area offers a diverse mix of terrain, ideal for gravel biking. You'll find routes winding through picturesque vineyards, extensive pine forests, and along the scenic Garonne River. The Canal de Garonne also provides well-maintained paths, often connecting to older railway lines like the Roger Lapébie Cycle Path, which are perfect for a varied gravel grinding experience. Expect gentle elevation changes, making it accessible for various activity levels.

Q: Are there any easy gravel bike trails suitable for beginners in Portets?
A: Yes, Portets has options for beginners. Many routes along the Garonne River and the Canal de Garonne feature relatively flat and well-maintained paths. An example of an easy route is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070197588/from-portets-to-bordeaux" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Portets to Bordeaux&lt;/a&gt;, which follows the Garonne River and offers scenic views with minimal elevation.

Q: What are some challenging gravel routes near Portets?
A: For those seeking a more challenging ride, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1070226198/from-cerons-to-bordeaux" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;From Cérons to Bordeaux&lt;/a&gt; offer varied terrain through the Graves appellation vineyards and along the river, with more significant elevation changes. Another option is the longer &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38572168" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Blue Lake – Blue Lake loop from Beautiran&lt;/a&gt;, which is classified as difficult and covers a substantial distance.

Q: Are there family-friendly gravel biking options in Portets?
A: Absolutely. The paths along the Canal de Garonne and sections of the Roger Lapébie Cycle Path are generally flat, well-maintained, and shaded, making them excellent choices for families. These routes offer a safe and enjoyable environment away from traffic, allowing for a relaxed cycling experience through charming villages and natural landscapes.

Q: What landmarks or attractions can I see along the gravel bike trails?
A: The region is rich in sights. You can cycle past historic vineyards of the Graves appellation, encounter ancient structures like the Tower of the King, and explore charming villages. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5601997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roger Lapébie Cycle Path&lt;/a&gt; itself is a major attraction, offering constantly changing panoramas. You might also find fitted water spots along the Garonne River, perfect for a break. For a natural highlight, consider visiting &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4637300" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Springs Pond&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for gravel biking in Portets?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally ideal for gravel biking in Portets. The weather is typically mild, and the landscapes are particularly beautiful, with blooming vineyards in spring and vibrant colors in autumn. Summer can be pleasant, but it's advisable to start early to avoid the midday heat. Winter rides are possible, but be prepared for cooler temperatures and potentially wetter conditions.

Q: Are there any circular gravel bike routes around Portets?
A: Yes, there are several loop options available. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26230987" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old railroad line – Church in La Brède loop from Saint-Médard-d'Eyrans&lt;/a&gt; offers a circular route through forests and agricultural areas. Another loop is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/26230950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church in La Brède – Le Saucats loop from Saint-Médard-d'Eyrans&lt;/a&gt;, providing another option for a round trip.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the gravel bike trails in Portets?
A: Many of the natural trails, especially those through forests and along riverbanks, are generally dog-friendly, provided your dog is well-behaved and kept under control. However, specific rules may apply in certain protected areas or private vineyard paths. It's always best to check local signage and ensure your dog is comfortable with the distance and terrain.

Q: Where can I find parking for gravel biking in Portets?
A: Parking is generally available in and around Portets, particularly near popular starting points for cycling routes or in village centers. For routes along the Canal de Garonne or the Roger Lapébie Cycle Path, you can often find designated parking areas at access points or in nearby towns. Public parking is usually well-indicated.

Q: Is public transport available to Portets with bikes?
A: Portets is accessible by regional train services, and many trains in France allow bikes, often with a small fee or during off-peak hours. It's recommended to check the specific train operator's policy and make reservations if necessary, especially for longer journeys or during busy periods. This allows you to easily reach starting points for your gravel adventures.

Q: What do other gravel bikers enjoy most about Portets?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 11 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, the peaceful rides through vineyards and forests, and the well-maintained paths along the Garonne River and Canal de Garonne. The blend of natural beauty and historical routes creates a rich and varied experience.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the gravel routes?
A: Yes, especially along the Canal de Garonne and in the charming villages you'll pass through, you'll find opportunities for refreshments. Many routes will lead you near local cafes, bakeries, or even vineyards offering tastings. For example, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5221735" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bistrot de la Pimpine&lt;/a&gt; is a highlight that could serve as a good stop.

Q: Do I need any special permits for gravel biking in the Gironde region?
A: Generally, no special permits are required for gravel biking on public paths, greenways, or designated cycling routes in the Gironde region, including around Portets. However, always respect private property signs, especially when cycling through vineyards, and adhere to any local regulations posted in natural parks or protected areas.
tips: This small tree-lined single will refresh you in the summer, but be careful in winter, the level can rise.
This track is ideal for avoiding a large rolling avenue with a lot of traffic and getting lost in the vineyards and woods.
This small stretch of path along the railway line is pleasant. The small marsh there is atypical. Be careful however in winter, it is often inaccessible.
